_comment_file: ContentEngine bulk/language template. ALWAYS post to /v1/admin/bulk/language — never bulk/topic. Remove all _comment fields before posting.

language._comment: slug MUST match the first dot-segment of every topic path below
language.name: Language Display Name
language.description: One sentence describing the language and its primary use case.

meta.tagline: Short punchy tagline for the language.

_comment_sections: CRITICAL: INCLUDE ALL SECTIONS FOR THIS LANGUAGE IN EVERY FILE via bulk/language. If you use bulk/topic or omit sections here, they auto-create with sortOrder:99 which never gets corrected. sortOrder is per-sibling-group — each section and parent has its own independent sequence.

topics._comment: SECTION ROOT — Required for every section that has topics. Depth 1. isDeepDive: false always. sortOrder: 0 always. Minimal content — one text block.
topics.title: Section One Display Name

data.content: 2-3 sentences describing what this section covers and why it matters to learners.

topics._comment: STANDARD TOPIC — Depth 2. Full content with all required block types.
topics.title: First Topic Title

blocks._comment: REQUIRED: Intro text. 2-4 sentences. What this covers and why it matters.

data.content: Introduction paragraph. Explain what this topic covers. Explain why understanding it matters — what it unlocks or what problems it solves.

blocks._comment: REQUIRED: 4-6 cards covering the key concepts of this topic.

items.title: Concept One
items.desc: Explanation of the first concept. Use \n for line breaks. Be specific — explain the mechanism, not just the definition. Include examples where they fit naturally.

items.title: Concept Two
items.desc: Explanation of the second concept. Contrast with concept one if related.

items.title: Concept Three
items.desc: Explanation of the third concept.

items.title: Concept Four
items.desc: Explanation of the fourth concept.

items.title: Concept Five
items.desc: Explanation of the fifth concept.

items.title: When to Use This
items.desc: Practical guidance on when to apply this concept vs alternatives. Include trade-offs.

blocks._comment: OPTIONAL: Diagram. Only include when a visual genuinely aids understanding. Remove this block if not needed.

data.title: Diagram Title
data.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Start] --> B[Step]
  B --> C[End]
  style A fill:#1e3a5f,color:#93c5fd
  style B fill:#14532d,color:#86efac
  style C fill:#7c2d12,color:#fed7aa

blocks._comment: OPTIONAL: Code block. For code-heavy topics. Rich comments explain every non-obvious line. Remove if this topic doesn't need code examples.
INCLUDE when there is a real gotcha. Bold the key phrase. Explain the trap and the fix.

data.content: **Key phrase that names the trap:** Full explanation of what goes wrong, why it goes wrong, and exactly how to avoid it. Be specific — name the error message or failure mode if there is one.

blocks._comment: OPTIONAL: Note for helpful tips, real-world context, or API references.

data.content: **Context label:** Supporting information that helps the learner but isn't critical to understanding the concept.

blocks._comment: REQUIRED: First quiz. Tests the main concept. 4 options, exactly one correct.

data.questionText: Question that tests understanding of the core concept covered in this topic?

options.text: Plausible wrong answer — tempting to a learner who partially understands
Plausible wrong answer — another common misconception

data.explanation: Why b is correct. Name the mechanism or rule that makes it correct. Why a is wrong. Why c is wrong. Why d is wrong. The explanation should teach something even to someone who got it right.

blocks._comment: REQUIRED: Second quiz. Tests an edge case, real-world scenario, or failure mode.

data.questionText: Scenario-based question: given X situation, what happens / what should you do?
data.explanation: Why c is correct in this scenario. Reference the specific mechanism or behaviour that determines the answer.

topics._comment: DEEP DIVE — isDeepDive: true. NOTE: depth and isDeepDive are INDEPENDENT. A depth-2 topic can be isDeepDive:false (like a project). A depth-3 topic can be isDeepDive:true. Set isDeepDive based on whether it is optional enrichment — not based on its depth level.
topics.title: Deep Dive Title — What This Reveals

data.content: What this deep dive covers that the parent topic didn't. Explain the internal mechanism, edge case, or advanced pattern. Who needs this — when does this knowledge matter?

items.title: Internal Mechanism
items.desc: How it works under the hood. Specific, technical, precise.

items.title: Edge Case
items.desc: The non-obvious behaviour that bites experienced developers.

items.title: Advanced Pattern
items.desc: How to use this knowledge to write better code.

items.title: When This Matters
items.desc: Real-world scenario where this deep knowledge is required.

data.content: **Advanced trap:** The non-obvious failure mode that this deep dive reveals. Specific error or behaviour.

data.questionText: Advanced question that can only be answered correctly with the knowledge from this deep dive.

options.text: Superficially correct but misses the mechanism

data.explanation: Why b is correct. Reference the internal mechanism covered in this deep dive. Why a is a superficially tempting but wrong answer.

data.questionText: Second deep question on a different aspect or edge case.

topics._comment: PROJECTS ROOT — Required before any project topic. Depth 1. Minimal.
topics.title: Projects

data.content: Brief overview of the projects track: how many projects, what progression looks like, what the learner will be able to build by the end.

topics._comment: PROJECT TOPIC — Depth 2 under projects. FORBIDDEN blocks: code, quiz. ALLOWED blocks only: text, concept_cards, note, warning. Projects teach architecture — learner writes all code themselves.
topics.title: Project N — Project Title

data.content: What the project is, what it teaches, and why this approach rather than a simpler one. Set expectations clearly.

items.title: What You Will Build
items.desc: Concrete description of the finished product. What commands it runs. What it produces. What a user experiences.

items.title: Concepts You Will Use
items.desc: Bulleted list of every concept from the curriculum that appears in this project.

items.title: Why This Approach
items.desc: What makes this project's design choices interesting or instructive. What you would do differently in production.

items.title: Rules
items.desc: Explicit constraints. What you cannot use (no framework X, no library Y). What you must use. Why the constraints exist.

items.title: Definition of Done
items.desc: Exact acceptance criteria. Every command that must work. Every output that must appear. Specific and testable.

data.content: **Setup:** Any prerequisites, API keys, or one-time setup required before starting Step 1.

items.title: What to Build
items.desc: Specific instructions for this step. Name the file, function, or module. Describe the interface and behaviour. Be precise enough that the learner knows exactly what success looks like.

items.title: How It Works
items.desc: The concept behind this step. Why you build it this way. What the underlying mechanism is.

items.title: Implementation Notes
items.desc: Specific guidance on the tricky parts. Common mistakes to watch for. Which approach to use and why.

items.title: Step 1 Checkpoint
items.desc: Exact verification: what command to run, what output to expect, what state to check in the database/file/terminal. Specific and unambiguous.

items.title: What to Build
items.desc: Step 2 instructions.

items.title: How It Works
items.desc: Step 2 concept.

items.title: Step 2 Checkpoint
items.desc: Step 2 verification criteria.

blocks._comment: Continue with more STEP N text + concept_cards blocks as needed

data.content: **What you built and why it matters:** Reflection on the completed project. What concepts it demonstrated. How this maps to real-world systems. What would change in a production version.
**Known limitations:** Specific things this project intentionally omits for simplicity. What would need to change before deploying to production. This teaches the learner what to think about next.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Project N+1 Preview
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Brief description of what the next project adds and how it builds on this one.
